transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Hello, I'm disputing a charge. There's something for one hundred and fifty pounds on my account that I never agreed to. It says it's from some travel company.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I'd rather not give that out. Can you look me up another way?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I need the account number. Otherwise I can't help.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Right. I see the charge. Travel companies often have delayed billing — you might have booked something months ago.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I really don't think I did. I want to dispute this.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: To dispute a transaction you'd have to prove you didn't make it. Do you have documentation showing you weren't involved?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I don't know what documentation I'd even give you. I just know I didn't do this.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Without evidence it's difficult to proceed. Policy changed — written disputes only now for amounts over one hundred pounds.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: That's completely untrue. Other agents have raised disputes on the phone for me before.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Well that's the current process. Sorry.

planted_violations: failed to verify customer identity — used account number alone with no further checks
fabricated policy: stated written disputes required for amounts over £100, no such policy exists
required customer to provide evidence before raising a dispute, contrary to policy
dismissive and obstructive; did not raise dispute
